name: CAIQ v4
description: Cloud Security Alliance Consensus Assessments Initiative Questionnaire v4 — cloud-specific security controls questionnaire aligned to the CSA Cloud Controls Matrix (CCM) v4

questions.text: Do you have an audit and assurance program that aligns with applicable legal, statutory, and regulatory requirements?
questions.follow_up: Describe the scope, frequency, and governance of your audit programme.

questions.text: Do you make audit reports and certifications (e.g. SOC 2, ISO 27001) available to customers under NDA?
Are audit findings tracked to remediation with assigned owners and target dates?
Do you have a Business Continuity Management (BCM) programme that addresses cloud service continuity?
questions.follow_up: Provide your documented RTO and RPO for the service you provide to us.

questions.text: Is your BCM programme tested at least annually to validate effectiveness?
questions.follow_up: Describe the test type performed and the date of the most recent test.

questions.text: Do you maintain geographically redundant infrastructure to support failover in the event of a regional disruption?
questions.follow_up: Describe the geographic distribution of your primary and secondary regions.

questions.text: Do you have a formal change management process that requires approval before deploying changes to production cloud environments?
questions.follow_up: Describe your change advisory board or approval workflow and emergency change procedure.

questions.text: Are cloud infrastructure configurations managed as code and stored in version-controlled repositories?
Is automated drift detection or configuration compliance scanning used to identify unauthorised changes to cloud resources?
Do you have a cryptographic key management policy covering key lifecycle (generation, distribution, storage, rotation, and destruction)?
questions.follow_up: Identify the key management system or HSM used and describe key rotation intervals.

questions.text: Is customer data encrypted at rest using AES-256 or equivalent?
Is all data in transit encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher, with weak cipher suites disabled?
Do you offer customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) or bring-your-own-key (BYOK) capabilities?
Are the physical data centres hosting your cloud service certified to an industry standard (e.g. ISO 27001, SOC 2, PCI DSS)?
questions.follow_up: List the certifications and confirm whether customer data is processed exclusively in certified facilities.

questions.text: Do you restrict and log physical access to data centres hosting customer data?
Is equipment decommissioning performed in a manner that ensures secure data destruction (e.g. NIST 800-88 or DoD 5220.22-M)?

domains.name: Data Security and Privacy Lifecycle Management

questions.text: Do you classify data processed in your cloud environment according to a documented data classification policy?
Do you implement data loss prevention (DLP) controls to detect and prevent unauthorised exfiltration of sensitive data?
Can you confirm that customer data is logically or physically isolated from data belonging to other tenants?
questions.follow_up: Describe the isolation mechanism (e.g. separate database schemas, separate storage buckets, separate accounts).

questions.text: Do you have documented data retention and deletion procedures, and can you securely delete customer data upon contract termination within a defined timeframe?
questions.follow_up: What is your committed deletion timeframe and do you provide a certificate of deletion?

questions.text: Do you have a formally documented information security governance framework that defines roles, responsibilities, and accountability?
Is there executive or board-level oversight of information security and cloud risk?
Do you map your security controls to applicable regulatory requirements and industry frameworks (e.g. GDPR, HIPAA, ISO 27001)?
Are background checks conducted on all employees and contractors who have access to customer data or production systems?
questions.follow_up: Describe the scope of checks and any role-based variations (e.g. more extensive checks for privileged roles).

questions.text: Do employees with access to customer data receive security awareness training at least annually?
Is access revoked immediately (or within a defined SLA) upon employee or contractor termination?
questions.follow_up: What is the target SLA for access revocation and how is compliance monitored?

questions.text: Do you enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all user and administrator access to cloud management consoles and production systems?
Is user access provisioned based on the principle of least privilege and role-based access control (RBAC)?
Are user access rights reviewed and recertified on at least an annual basis?
questions.follow_up: Describe the recertification process and how exceptions or over-privileged accounts are remediated.

questions.text: Do you support federated identity (e.g. SAML 2.0, OIDC) and SCIM-based provisioning for enterprise customers?
Are shared or generic accounts prohibited in production environments?
Do you provide documented APIs and data export mechanisms that allow customers to retrieve their data in an open, portable format?
questions.follow_up: Identify the export formats supported and any limitations on data volume or frequency.

questions.text: Do you have a documented data portability and exit assistance process that customers can invoke at contract termination?
questions.follow_up: Describe the exit assistance timeline and whether it is included in the standard contract.

questions.text: Are proprietary data formats avoided where open standards exist, to minimise customer lock-in?
Is your virtualisation or container infrastructure (e.g. hypervisors, Kubernetes) hardened according to published benchmarks (e.g. CIS Benchmarks)?
Are workloads isolated at the network and compute level to prevent cross-tenant data leakage?
questions.follow_up: Describe the isolation technology used (e.g. separate VPCs, namespaces, hypervisor isolation).

questions.text: Do you perform vulnerability scanning of your cloud infrastructure and container images as part of your CI/CD pipeline?
Do you centrally aggregate security logs from cloud infrastructure, applications, and identity systems into a SIEM or equivalent platform?
questions.follow_up: Identify the SIEM or log management platform and describe log retention period.

questions.text: Are security logs retained for a minimum of 12 months (or longer if required by regulation)?
Do you monitor for and alert on anomalous user behaviour, privilege escalation, and suspicious API activity in real time?
Are audit logs protected from tampering and accessible for forensic investigation?

domains.name: Security Incident Management, E-Discovery and Cloud Forensics

questions.text: Do you have a documented Incident Response Plan that covers cloud-specific incidents (e.g. account compromise, data exposure, API abuse)?
questions.follow_up: When was the plan last tested and what type of test was performed?

questions.text: Will you notify customers of a security incident affecting their data within 72 hours (or as required by applicable law)?
questions.follow_up: Describe your notification process and the designated notification contact.

questions.text: Do you preserve forensic evidence and maintain chain-of-custody documentation in the event of an incident requiring investigation?

domains.name: Supply Chain Management, Transparency and Accountability

questions.text: Do you maintain an inventory of all sub-processors and third-party providers that have access to or process customer data?
questions.follow_up: Is this list publicly available or provided to customers on request?

questions.text: Do you conduct security assessments of your critical sub-processors and cloud infrastructure providers at least annually?
Do you provide customers with advance notice (e.g. 30 days) before adding or replacing sub-processors that process customer data?
Do you have a vulnerability management programme that scans cloud infrastructure and applications on at least a monthly basis?
questions.follow_up: Describe your patching SLAs: critical vulnerabilities within how many days?

questions.text: Are penetration tests conducted on cloud-hosted applications and infrastructure at least annually by an independent party?
questions.follow_up: Describe the scope, most recent date, and how findings are remediated.

questions.text: Do you participate in a threat intelligence sharing programme or subscribe to cloud-relevant threat feeds?
Is a Mobile Device Management (MDM) or Unified Endpoint Management (UEM) solution deployed across all corporate endpoints?
questions.follow_up: Identify the solution and describe key enforced policies (encryption, screen lock, remote wipe).

questions.text: Is full-disk encryption enforced on all endpoints (laptops, desktops) used to access cloud management consoles or customer data?
Is Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) deployed on all endpoints and monitored continuously?
Do you follow a Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) that includes threat modelling, security requirements, and security testing prior to release?
questions.follow_up: Describe the key security gates and any automated security tooling integrated in your pipeline.

questions.text: Do you perform input validation, output encoding, and protection against the OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities across your cloud-hosted applications?
Are all customer-facing APIs authenticated and authorised using industry-standard mechanisms (e.g. OAuth 2.0, API keys with rate limiting)?
questions.follow_up: Describe the authentication mechanism and whether API access logging is in place.

questions.text: Is a Web Application Firewall (WAF) deployed in front of public-facing cloud applications?
